column2: THE COMMITTEE ON THE ELIMINATION OF
General recommendation No. 5: Temporary special measures
The Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women,
Taking note that the reports, the introductory remarks and the replies by States parties
reveal that while significant progress has been achieved in regard to repealing or modifying
discriminatory laws, there is still a need for action to be taken to implement fully the Convention
by introducing measures to promote de facto equality between men and women,
Recommends that States parties make more use of temporary special measures such as
positive action, preferential treatment or quota systems to advance women’s integration into
education, the economy, politics and employment.
